core_mechanism: Classification of the boundary/interface fixed points of a scale-invariant (critical) system by the irreducible representations of its infinite-dimensional conformal symmetry algebra, together with a monotonic 'boundary entropy' functional that orders the renormalization-group flows between those fixed points.
brief_reason: The abstract identifies boundary-condition-changing operators, Virasoro characters, and conformally invariant partition functions, computes the entropies of the boundary states, and organizes the flows between boundary critical points into a phase diagram — i.e. it rests on conformal-symmetry representation theory plus a boundary-entropy monotone on RG flows, with the explicit solutions generated by the integrable boundary Yang-Baxter (reflection) equations.
